prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of formaldehyde (4-atom system: 1 C, 1 O, 2 H) using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, singlet multiplicity. Geometry held fixed at input coordinates. Fukui functions computed from Mulliken charges of neutral parent, +1 cation (doublet), and −1 anion (doublet). All SCF computations converged. The carbon atom emerges as the most electrophilic site (f⁺ = 0.7084), and oxygen as the most nucleophilic (f⁻ = 0.4674). The dual descriptor (Δf) is dominated by carbon's electrophilic character (+0.5959), while hydrogens carry nucleophilic character (−0.298 each). Charge conservation checks (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000) confirm convergence. Integrity status: trustworthy. Live calculation log streamed to the task's `.out` file during execution. Bar chart visualization of per-atom indices generated and saved.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
